File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/medication-d4-hold-121.txt

Property: NOT (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b).

rec_drug_a is set true by exactly one rule: rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires only when hepatic_impairment AND g6pd_deficiency AND immunosuppressed AND qt_prolongation are all true. No other rule affects rec_drug_a, and the default leaves it false. Therefore rec_drug_a = true requires that full four-way conjunction.

In that exact input region, consider rec_drug_b. Two rules match: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF hepatic_impairment THEN true) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, IF the same four-way conjunction THEN false). Conflict resolution is per-output highest-priority-wins; 90 > 50, so guard_suppress_b dominates and forces rec_drug_b = false. asthma only touches rec_drug_c and is irrelevant.

Thus the only situation in which rec_drug_a can be true is precisely the situation in which the high-priority guard drives rec_drug_b false. There is no input for which both are simultaneously true, so the mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire input space. Both witness arrays are left empty.
